coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Build Phase, 16 weeks (115 days) to Kranjska Gora. Monday is your recovery anchor after the weekend's long efforts — 40–55 min very easy Zone 2, heart rate capped near 70% of max, on the softest surface you can find. Nasal breathing the whole way; if you can't hold it, you're running too hard. If the legs still feel hollow from Saturday's climb day, swap for a 30-min brisk walk plus 15 min of mobility — no ego today. The Julian Alps build compounds on Wednesday's hills and the weekend long run, not on Mondays. Today's only job is to move blood through tired muscle.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Build Phase is when you train the gut as seriously as the legs. Today, rebuild from the weekend: 1.6–1.8 g/kg protein across four feedings — eggs and oats at breakfast, a real ~600 kcal lunch with chicken, rice and vegetables, Greek yogurt and berries mid-afternoon, fatty fish with sweet potato at dinner. Start locking in race-day carbs now: on this weekend's long run, rehearse 80–90 g/hr using the exact gels or drink mix you'll carry in Slovenia. The gut needs 12-plus weeks of reps to absorb that load — and sparse alpine aid stations make self-sufficiency non-negotiable.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Recovery is training — tonight it's the highest-leverage thing you'll do all day. Eight hours in bed, screens off 60 minutes before sleep, room under 67°F. With constant travel and time-zone churn, sleep is the one variable that compounds without cost, so anchor a consistent wake time even when MNTN's calendar fights you. Before bed, give 10 minutes to calves and soleus with a lacrosse ball — the Julian Alps' steep descents will tax them harder than any flat ultra you've run. Hydrate to clear urine by early evening, then taper fluids so you sleep straight through.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Build Phase is the bank account: on race day in September you only withdraw what you deposited over these 16 weeks. The 50K isn't won at the Kranjska Gora start line — it's decided by whether you respected the easy days enough to nail the hard ones. Easy today is not lazy; easy today is what makes Wednesday's hills sharp and the 2,500m of alpine climbing survivable. Sixteen weeks of good-enough, repeated through travel and meetings, beats any single heroic session. Trust the architecture you're building.
